---
title: "Reaching credentialed sources: the broker pattern"
description: "One rule: the LLM never touches the secret. A non-LLM broker holds the token and returns only data. SSH ControlMaster is a working in-house instance."
date: "2026-07-19"
categories: [field-notes, identity]
draft: true
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status"}
**Partly built.** The load-bearing shape is demonstrated; the general version is next.
:::

**What happened.** It is the present-day form of the control-plane / data-plane split, and it is DID-ready at the same seam: swap the OAuth token for a verifiable credential later.

**Why it matters.** One rule: the LLM never touches the secret. A non-LLM broker holds the token and returns only data. SSH ControlMaster is a working in-house instance.

**Details.** Drawn from the wiki's Agent-Source-Auth. Honest limit: ControlMaster is a coarse, campus-local broker, not a scoped entitlement.
